user_request: coba deh liat apa yang kurang dan bsa di improve dri diagram nya, bukan masalah isi nya ato field nya, ini lebih ke secara akademis nya.

content:
User wants academic review of 7 class diagrams under `diagram/ai/Class/`. NOT about content/fields — academic UML quality.

Scope split:
- Naomi: audit actual drawio files. Check UML notation completeness: visibility markers (+/-/#), multiplicity labels on relations, role names, generalization/inheritance, abstract classes, enums, dependency vs association distinction, navigation arrows, layout/readability. Compare against UML 2.x standard for thesis-level CD.
- Yamamoto: strategic review of academic gaps. Indonesia thesis (TA) conventions: caption format, layering (boundary/control/entity), package diagrams, abstract+interface notation, enum types, constraints/notes. What's missing for thesis-grade CD?

Both parallel. After return: synthesize into improvement list. Deliver to user — they can pick what to act on.

Files:
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_MANAJEMEN_USER.drawio (39 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN.drawio (33 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_MANAJEMEN_PROYEK.drawio (82 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_MANAJEMEN_TUGAS.drawio (92 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_BUDGET_MATERIAL.drawio (63 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_ESKALASI.drawio (69 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/CD_SISTEM.drawio (279 cells)
- diagram/ai/Class/_gen.py (generator)
- diagram/STANDARD.md (style rules)

note: Reading _gen.py generator script + STANDARD.md + spot-check one .drawio file
Loading diagram artifacts + STANDARD.md + TODO.md to ground academic strategic review

summary: Generator source-of-truth for all 7 class diagrams: class defs, edge styles, multiplicity, captions
Style baseline: global rules, caption format, relationship styles, caption numbering
Spot-check file: confirms generator output exactly matches drawio XML; 39 cells total
Generator script: 6 per-fitur + 1 sistem CD. Domain-only entity layer. No boundary/control/stereotype/abstraction.
Style guide: white/black, italic captions, Gambar 4.X numbering. NO class diagram section yet.
Bab 4 outline: 4.3.1.4 Model Class Diagram is single subsection. 4.3.2 Perancangan Model Data follows.
11 tables. Constraint checks (status enums, role enums, SPI thresholds via health_status_check). All visible in CD attrs but constraints not annotated as UML notes.

content: ## Audit Scope Source: _gen.py (lines 1-514) = canonical definition of all 7 diagrams. Spot-check: CD_MANAJEMEN_USER.drawio confirms generator output is faithfully reproduced (XML matches exactly). 7 diagrams: CD_MANAJEMEN_USER, CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN, CD_MANAJEMEN_PROYEK, CD_MANAJEMEN_TUGAS, CD_BUDGET_MATERIAL, CD_ESKALASI, CD_SISTEM. --- ## Checklist Results (12 items) ### 1. Visibility Markers — OK All attributes prefixed `- ` (private), all methods prefixed `+ ` (public). Evidence: _gen.py lines 121-127 (USER_FULL attrs), 124-127 (USER_FULL methods). `- id: int {PK}`, `+ login(email, password): User` — consistent across all 14 class definitions. No missing markers found. No `#` (protected) or `~` (package) used, but none are warranted given the flat domain model. ### 2. Type Annotations — OK (with 1 caveat) Attributes: `name: Type` pattern used throughout. `string`, `int`, `decimal`, `boolean`, `date`, `timestamp`, `float` all present. Methods: `name(args): ReturnType` pattern used. Return types include `void`, `User`, `User[]`, `Project[]`, `float`, `decimal`, `boolean`, `File`, `TaskEvidence[]`, `AuditLog[]`, etc. CAVEAT: Method args are sometimes untyped (`+ create(data): Client`, `+ log(message, type): void`). "data" and "type" are not typed — acceptable in academic class diagrams at this level of abstraction, but a strict UML 2.x reader would note them. Evidence: _gen.py lines 143-148 (CLIENT_FULL methods), 225-227 (TaskActivity methods). ### 3. Multiplicity on Relationships — OK All edges carry explicit source (ms) and target (mt) multiplicity labels. Evidence: _gen.py edge() function signature `edge(eid, src, tgt, style, ms, mt, role=None, ...)` — ms/mt always provided. Values used: `"1"`, `"*"`, `"0..1"`, `"1..*"`, `"1"/"1"` (one-to-one). Drawio verification: CD_MANAJEMEN_USER.drawio lines 107-112 shows `rel_user_audit_lms value="1"` and `rel_user_audit_lmt value="*"` both rendered as edge label cells. No unlabeled lines found. ### 4. Role Names on Associations — PARTIAL Role names ARE used on dependency/dashed associations (`S_ASSOC_DASH`) but NOT on composition/aggregation edges. Edges WITH roles: - rel_user_audit: `role="changed_by"` (_gen.py line 318) - rel_user_client: `role="created_by"` (_gen.py line 333) - rel_user_task: `role="assigned_to"` (_gen.py line 383) - rel_user_escalation: `role="reported_by / resolved_by"` (_gen.py line 441) - rel_user_audit (CD_SISTEM): `role="changed_by"` (_gen.py line 509) Edges WITHOUT roles (all S_COMP and S_AGGR): - rel_project_assignment, rel_project_health, rel_task_evidence, rel_task_activity, rel_project_material, rel_project_budget, rel_project_dailyreport, rel_escalation_update, and all S_ASSOC associations (rel_user_assignment, rel_project_assignment). Role names on associations (solid line, S_ASSOC) like `rel_user_assignment` and `rel_project_assignment` are absent. These carry semantic meaning (user "participates_in" project, project "contains" assignment) that role names would clarify. SEVERITY: Moderate. Academic reviewers typically expect role names on named associations. The navigability direction is implied but not labeled. ### 5. Relationship Types Differentiated — PARTIAL Types defined in _gen.py lines 23-28: - S_ASSOC: plain solid line, no arrows (`endArrow=none;startArrow=none`) — OK for bidirectional - S_ASSOC_DASH: dashed variant of S_ASSOC (`dashed=1`) — used for dependency-type links (User->AuditLog, User->Task) - S_AGGR: hollow diamond (`endArrow=diamondThin;endFill=0`) — used for Client->Project (aggregation) - S_COMP: filled diamond (`endArrow=diamondThin;endFill=1`) — used for owned parts (Task->Evidence, Project->Health, etc.) GAPS: a) GENERALIZATION (inheritance) — NOT PRESENT. No `endArrow=block;endFill=0` (open triangle on solid line) style defined. There are no inheritance hierarchies in the diagrams. The domain model has no class hierarchy (no abstract base classes, no User subclass hierarchy). This is a content choice, not a notation error per se, but a reviewer will notice there is no generalization example in any of the 7 diagrams. b) REALIZATION — NOT PRESENT. No `dashed` + `endArrow=block;endFill=0` style defined or used. Again, no interfaces implemented. c) DEPENDENCY — S_ASSOC_DASH is used where formal UML dependency (`dashed arrow with open arrowhead`) should appear. S_ASSOC_DASH has `endArrow=none` (no arrowhead at all). A dependency arrow requires `endArrow=open` or `endArrow=classic`. The User->AuditLog relationship (uses-for-logging) is directional — missing arrowhead on dashed line. Evidence: _gen.py line 24: `S_ASSOC_DASH = S_ASSOC + "dashed=1;"` where `S_ASSOC = "endArrow=none;startArrow=none;..."` — no arrow on dashed edges. SEVERITY: High (item c). The dashed lines used for dependency/uses relationships have no arrowhead, violating UML 2.x dependency notation. Items a/b are domain-driven absences (acceptable if no inheritance exists). ### 6. Abstract Classes / Interfaces — GAP No abstract classes defined. No `fontStyle=3` (italic name) applied to any class header. The `S_CLASS` style uses `fontStyle=1` (bold only, _gen.py line 7) — no italic variant for abstract classes. No `<>` stereotype used anywhere. No abstract class or interface is present across all 7 diagrams. SEVERITY: Low if domain truly has no abstract concepts. However, academic thesis reviewers often expect at least one example or a note explaining why none exist (e.g., "domain is purely concrete entity model"). Worth noting in thesis commentary. ### 7. Enums — PARTIAL Enum-typed attributes ARE labeled with `: enum` type annotation (e.g., `- role: enum`, `- status: enum`, `- phase: enum`, `- priority: enum`, `- file_type: enum`). BUT: No separate `<>` class is defined anywhere in the 7 diagrams. The enum values (`to_do|working_on_it|done`, `technician|manager|admin`, `survey|execution`, etc.) are described in CLAUDE.md but not materialized as drawio enum class cells. Academic UML standard: status enums should be shown as a separate class box with `<>` stereotype header and literal values as attributes (e.g., `TaskStatus <>: to_do, working_on_it, done`). Evidence: All `enum` references in _gen.py class definitions (lines 122, 155, 162-163, 191, 207, 231, 239, 259, 261-263) point to inline `: enum` text, no enum class cells instantiated. SEVERITY: High. Missing `<>` classes is a standard academic gap. Thesis examiners specifically look for this. ### 8. Stereotypes — PARTIAL The caption function uses `Gambar 4.X ...` (_gen.py line 96) — correct italic HTML. `<>` stereotype: ABSENT (see item 7). `<>` stereotype: NOT USED. No class header carries `<>` or `<>` or `<>` stereotypes. Plain class boxes with bold name only. `<>` stereotype: ABSENT (see item 6). The only stereotype-like notation present is constraint syntax `{PK}`, `{FK}`, `{unique}`, `{derived}` in attribute values — these are UML constraints, not stereotypes, but they demonstrate awareness of UML annotation conventions. SEVERITY: Moderate. `<>` is optional in many styles. `<>` absence (item 7) is the critical gap. A reviewer expecting 3-tier architecture-aware stereotypes will note absence of `<>` / `<>` / `<>` but this is domain-specific. ### 9. Notes / Constraints (UML note boxes) — GAP No UML note cells (yellow folded-corner rectangles) are defined in any of the 7 diagrams. Business rules that should appear as notes: - SPI threshold rules: Green >= 0.95, Amber 0.85-0.95, Red < 0.85 (critical for thesis — this is the EWS core logic) - Computed fields: `total_price: decimal {derived}` exists in MATERIAL (_gen.py line 233) but no note explains the formula `quantity * unit_price` - Role permission constraints: Technician cannot mark tasks `done` (no note on Task class) - Overtime/OverDeadline computed logic (not stored in DB, purely computed) — not documented in any diagram Evidence: Search of _gen.py reveals zero `shape=note` or `callout` style cells. No `S_NOTE` style defined. SEVERITY: High for academic quality. The EWS thresholds and computed state logic are the thesis's CORE CONTRIBUTION. Not documenting them in the diagrams is a missed opportunity to demonstrate academic rigor. ### 10. Layout / Readability — OK (with caveats per diagram) Simple diagrams (CD_MANAJEMEN_USER: 2 classes, 1 edge) are clean. Medium diagrams have routing hints via `exit_xy` / `entry_xy` / explicit `points` waypoints (_gen.py lines 71-73, 381-382, etc.). Potential issues in CD_SISTEM (most complex, 13 classes on 1700x1400 canvas, _gen.py lines 453-512): - 13 classes with 11 edges. Left-to-right + top-bottom partial flow but classes at varying heights (y=60 for top row, y=460-940 for lower rows). May have line crossings — cannot verify without rendering, but coordinate inspection shows potential crossing between rel_project_material (points through y=720) and rel_project_budget/dailyreport lines. - REF classes (USER_REF, CLIENT_REF, PROJECT_REF, TASK_REF) are visually indistinguishable from FULL classes — same `S_CLASS` style, just fewer rows. SEVERITY: Low for individual diagrams, Moderate for CD_SISTEM. REF class ambiguity is item 12. ### 11. Caption — OK Caption generated via _gen.py lines 95-99, 300: `f"Gambar 4.X {title_indo}."` with `...` HTML wrapper. Style: `fontStyle=2` (italic), `fontSize=11`, centered. Format matches STANDARD.md exactly: `Gambar X.X Nama Diagram.` with period. The `4.X` placeholder (all 7 use `4.X`) is correct for generation-time — expected to be renumbered at final thesis assembly. Not a notation gap. Evidence: _gen.py line 300 and drawio line 116. ### 12. Full vs REF Class Differentiation — GAP REF classes (USER_REF, CLIENT_REF, PROJECT_REF, TASK_REF) are used across 6 of 7 diagrams to avoid repeating full class detail. Current: REF classes use identical `S_CLASS` style as FULL classes. No visual distinction — same white fill, same stroke, same bold header font. A reader of CD_MANAJEMEN_PROYEK sees two "User" boxes (one is REF in that diagram, one is FULL in CD_MANAJEMEN_USER) with no indication that User_REF is a subset/proxy. Academic convention: REF/stub classes should use either: (a) dashed border, (b) `<>` or `<>` stereotype, (c) lighter fill color (e.g., `#F5F5F5`), or (d) `...` as final attribute row indicating truncation. Evidence: _gen.py lines 129-133 (USER_REF) vs 119-127 (USER_FULL) — both passed to `cls()` function with same `S_CLASS` style, no variant style for REF. SEVERITY: Moderate. A thesis examiner may ask: "Why does User appear with 3 fields in one diagram and 7 in another?" Without visual cue, this reads as inconsistency rather than deliberate abstraction. --- ## Summary Table | # | Item | Status | Severity | |---|------|--------|----------| | 1 | Visibility markers (+/-/#/~) | OK | — | | 2 | Type annotations (name: Type, method(): Return) | OK (untyped args caveat) | Low | | 3 | Multiplicity on all edges | OK | — | | 4 | Role names on associations | PARTIAL (only on dashed/dependency edges; absent on ASSOC/COMP) | Moderate | | 5 | Relationship types differentiated | PARTIAL (dashed dependency missing arrowhead; no generalization/realization) | High | | 6 | Abstract classes / interfaces | GAP (none exist; domain is flat entity model) | Low | | 7 | Enum classes with <> | GAP (inline `: enum` only; no enum class cells) | High | | 8 | Stereotypes (<>, <>, <>) | PARTIAL (constraints present; stereotypes absent) | Moderate | | 9 | UML notes/constraints for business rules | GAP (zero note boxes; SPI thresholds, computed states undocumented) | High | | 10 | Layout / readability | OK (caveats on CD_SISTEM complexity) | Low-Moderate | | 11 | Caption format | OK | — | | 12 | Full vs REF class visual distinction | GAP (identical style; no visual cue for subset classes) | Moderate | --- ## Critical Findings (High Severity — 3 items) ### F1: Dashed dependency edges have no arrowhead (_gen.py:24) S_ASSOC_DASH inherits `endArrow=none` from S_ASSOC. UML 2.x dependency is a dashed line with open arrowhead. Current: dashed line, no arrow. Affected: User->AuditLog (CD_MANAJEMEN_USER), User->Client (CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN), User->Task (CD_MANAJEMEN_TUGAS), User->Escalation (CD_ESKALASI), User->AuditLog (CD_SISTEM). ### F2: No <> classes (_gen.py:119-287 — all class defs) 9+ enum-typed attributes across 14 class definitions. None materialized as separate enum class. Status enums (Task status, User role, Project phase/status, etc.) should each be an `<>` box. This is a structural gap visible to any UML-trained examiner. ### F3: No UML note boxes for business rules (_gen.py:1-514 — entire file) Zero note cells. SPI calculation formula, health thresholds, computed overtime/overdeadline logic, role-permission constraints are all absent from diagram notation. These are the thesis's academic contribution — not documenting them in diagrams weakens the design chapter.

summary: S_ASSOC_DASH has endArrow=none — dashed dependency edges missing arrowhead, violates UML 2.x dependency notation
Zero <<enumeration>> class cells across all 7 diagrams — 9+ enum attrs use inline ': enum' only, no separate enum class boxes
Zero UML note cells in all 7 diagrams — SPI thresholds, computed overtime/overdeadline logic, role-permission constraints undocumented
REF classes (USER_REF, CLIENT_REF, PROJECT_REF, TASK_REF) use identical S_CLASS style as FULL classes — no visual distinction for subset/proxy classes
ASSOC edges (rel_user_assignment, rel_project_assignment) have no role names — navigability direction implicit only

name: Diagram-type fit vs Indonesia TA convention
summary: Indonesia TA section 4.3.1.4 'Model Class Diagram' typically expects ONE comprehensive class diagram of the system as Gambar utama, optionally supplemented by per-fitur fragments. Current 6+1 split is non-standard but defensible IF sistem overview is positioned as Gambar 4.X (primary) and per-fitur as Gambar 4.X.1..6 (supplementary). The TODO.md shows 4.3.1.4 as a single subsection -- so the binding question is: does 'Model Class Diagram' appear once or as nested sub-subsections? With Recayasa Perangkat Lunak / Sistem Informasi convention at UTY, ONE primary CD is standard.

name: Layer pattern -- entity-only vs MVC stereotypes
summary: Current diagrams are pure ENTITY layer (domain model). For TA-grade Sistem Informasi, two acceptable conventions exist: (A) Domain-only CD (acceptable, common in IS thesis where 'Model Class Diagram' = data model abstraction), (B) Three-tier with <<boundary>>, <<control>>, <<entity>> stereotypes (RUP/ICONIX style, more rigorous, common in Teknik Informatika thesis). Current work picks (A). NOT a defect per se -- but author MUST justify choice in narrative or examiner will ask.

name: Generalization gap -- User role hierarchy missing
summary: schema.sql:14 has CHECK constraint role IN ('technician','manager','admin'). All 3 roles use SAME User class with role: enum attribute. UML convention: when behavior diverges by role (technician CANNOT mark done, manager CAN approve, admin manages users) -- this is a Generalization opportunity. Abstract User -> Technician/Manager/Admin subclasses with role-specific methods (markDone() only on Manager, manageUsers() only on Admin). Currently markDone() is on Task class -- which loses the role-locked semantics. Examiner question certainty: HIGH.

name: Business rule annotation gap
summary: Critical domain invariants not in diagrams: (a) SPI thresholds Green>=0.95 / Amber 0.85-0.95 / Red<0.85 (CLAUDE.md health logic), (b) overtime = working_on_it AND due_date<today (computed), (c) over_deadline = to_do AND due_date<today (computed), (d) survey-phase gate before execution. UML standard: attach as Note (sticky) to ProjectHealth and Task with {constraint} or OCL syntax. Current diagrams have ZERO notes. Major academic gap -- domain rules invisible.

name: Cardinality + association direction completeness
summary: Spot-check of _gen.py edge calls: ALL edges have ms+mt multiplicity (good). Composition vs Aggregation usage looks correct (Project COMPOSES Task/Material/Budget -- delete cascades; Client AGGREGATES Project -- ON DELETE SET NULL per schema.sql:59). One issue: associations are bidirectional via S_ASSOC (no arrows). For TA rigor, navigability arrows on associations help readability -- e.g. User -> AuditLog should show direction (User causes AuditLog, not vice versa).

name: Caption convention compliance
summary: STANDARD.md:34 mandates 'Gambar X.X Nama Diagram.' (period at end). _gen.py:300 emits 'Gambar 4.X {title}.' -- placeholder X is INTENTIONAL stub (numbering filled when inserted into Bab 4). Compliant. Italic via fontStyle=2 confirmed. ONE issue: title 'Class Diagram Sistem Dashboard PT Smart Home Inovasi' is verbose. TA convention prefers shorter, e.g. 'Class Diagram Sistem'. Per-fitur titles 'Class Diagram Manajemen X' are fine.

name: Method signature realism
summary: Some method signatures are RPC-flavored not OO. Examples: User class has + login(email, password): User AND + authenticate(token): User AND + register(data): User -- these are AuthService methods, not User instance methods. Pure UML: User class instance methods should be + setRole(role), + deactivate(), + getName(). Authentication operations belong in <<control>> AuthController/AuthService class. This is a symptom of Gap #2 (no control layer). For TA-grade rigor: either justify pragmatic mixing OR introduce control classes.

name: Cross-diagram consistency
summary: REF variants (USER_REF, PROJECT_REF, etc.) intentionally show STRIPPED attribute lists for cross-fitur references -- this is acceptable practice (focus diagram on owning fitur). BUT examiner may question: 'Why does User in CD_MANAJEMEN_USER have 7 attrs but User in CD_ESKALASI has 3?' Answer: REF stubs to avoid clutter. This MUST be stated in caption or text annotation, otherwise reads as inconsistency. Recommendation: add italic note 'Atribut yang ditampilkan adalah subset relevan untuk fitur ini' under per-fitur captions.

name: Missing TA-Bab-4 diagram types
summary: TODO.md sequence: 4.3.1.4 (Class) -> 4.3.2 (Perancangan Model Data) -> 4.3.3 (Perancangan Fisik Basis Data) -> 4.3.4 (Relasi Antar Data) -> 4.3.5 (Antarmuka). NO Package Diagram, NO Component Diagram, NO Deployment Diagram explicitly listed. Indonesia TA convention varies by department: SI typically requires only Class+ERD; TI may require Package/Deployment. UTY-specific: check thesis template. Verdict: NICE-TO-HAVE not required IF TODO.md is the binding outline. ERD will be 4.3.2-4.3.4 (separate from class diagrams).

name: Restructure to single primary CD
pros: Standard Indonesia TA convention (one canonical class diagram)
CD_SISTEM.drawio already exists -- just promote it
Examiner-friendly: single source of truth for system structure
Per-fitur fragments demoted to optional appendix
cons: Loses per-fitur readability for thesis chapter narrative
CD_SISTEM is dense (13 classes, 12 relations) at 1700x1400 -- may not fit single page

name: Keep hybrid: 1 sistem + 6 per-fitur as numbered subset
pros: Maps cleanly to per-fitur Bab 4 narrative structure
cons: Requires explicit caption hierarchy: Gambar 4.X (sistem) + Gambar 4.X.1..6 (per-fitur)
Need text justification why split is used
Some examiners still expect ONE primary diagram

name: Add three-tier stereotypes <<boundary>><<control>><<entity>>
cons: Doubles diagram complexity (need View/Controller classes added)
Code reality is Express routes + React components, not classical MVC -- forced fit
Adds 2-3 weeks of diagram work for marginal grade improvement
Sistem Informasi thesis often accepts entity-only

name: Add UML Notes for business rules
pros: Critical domain invariants become diagram-visible (SPI thresholds, role permissions, computed states)
Examiner immediately sees system rules without reading prose
Low effort -- ~6 notes total across CD_SISTEM and key per-fitur
cons: Need OCL or pseudo-syntax convention -- pick natural-language Indonesian

name: Add User generalization (abstract User + Technician/Manager/Admin)
pros: Resolves role-locked behavior questions (technician cannot markDone)
Standard UML pattern, examiners expect to see it for role-based systems
Maps to CHECK constraint in schema (role enum)
Method placement becomes natural: markDone() on Manager class only
cons: Code reality: single users table with role column, not separate tables -- generalization is conceptual not physical
Requires note explaining 'inheritance is logical, not physical (single-table inheritance)'

summary: RECOMMENDED PATH: Keep hybrid structure (1 sistem + 6 per-fitur). Add UML Notes for business rules (SPI, computed states, role gates). Add User generalization. SKIP three-tier stereotypes -- not worth the complexity for SI thesis. Add navigability arrows on key associations. Annotate REF stub convention in per-fitur captions or accompanying text.
Detailed prioritized improvement list
name: improvements

content:
## Strategic Verdict: IMPROVE (not RESTRUCTURE)

Current 7-file structure is defensible. Domain-only entity-layer choice is acceptable for Sistem Informasi thesis at UTY. But 4 academic gaps need closing before submission.

---

## Prioritized Gap Map

### CRITICAL (must-fix before submission, examiner will challenge)

**G1. User role generalization missing**
- Schema: `role IN ('technician','manager','admin')` with role-locked behavior (CLAUDE.md project memory entry 2026-04-04 score-16: "Technician CANNOT mark tasks 'done'")
- UML expects: abstract User -> Technician / Manager / Admin subclasses
- Fix: Add to CD_MANAJEMEN_USER and CD_SISTEM. Note "logical inheritance, single-table physical (role column)".
- Effort: small (3 new class boxes + generalization arrows)
- Academic impact: HIGH -- demonstrates OO modeling literacy

**G2. Business rules not annotated as UML Notes**
- Missing on diagrams: SPI thresholds (>=0.95 green, 0.85-0.95 amber, <0.85 red), overtime computed (status=working_on_it AND due_date<today), over_deadline computed (status=to_do AND due_date<today), survey-phase gate
- These are the THESIS CONTRIBUTION (EWS via SPI is the system's reason for existing per CLAUDE.md problem statement)
- Fix: 4-6 UML notes attached to ProjectHealth, Task, Project classes. Use Indonesian natural language: "{constraint} status=red jika spi_value < 0.85"
- Effort: small (sticky note shapes + dashed connector to anchored class)
- Academic impact: VERY HIGH -- without these, the diagram doesn't show the unique system contribution

**G3. Caption numbering placeholder unfilled**
- Current: "Gambar 4.X" literal in all captions
- Reality: numbering depends on insertion order in Bab 4 prose
- Fix: When integrating to thesis, run sed/find-replace to assign actual numbers. Establish convention now: CD_SISTEM = 4.X primary, per-fitur = 4.X.1..6 in order (User, Klien, Proyek, Tugas, Budget&Material, Eskalasi)
- Effort: trivial mechanical
- Academic impact: HIGH (uncorrected = unprofessional submission)

### IMPORTANT (strong recommend, defendable but weaker without)

**G4. RPC-style methods on entity classes**
- User.login(), User.authenticate() are service operations not entity behavior
- Either: (a) remove them from User and add brief sentence in Bab 4 narrative "operasi otentikasi diimplementasikan oleh layer service yang tidak digambarkan terpisah", OR (b) move to a single AuthService control class shown only in CD_MANAJEMEN_USER
- Pick (a) for minimum change. Domain-only justification holds.
- Effort: small (prune 2-3 methods per class)
- Academic impact: MEDIUM (examiner literacy-dependent)

**G5. REF stub variants need explicit acknowledgment**
- USER_REF in CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN shows 3 attrs; USER_FULL in CD_MANAJEMEN_USER shows 7
- Looks like inconsistency unless explained
- Fix: Add italic subtitle line under each per-fitur caption: "Atribut yang ditampilkan adalah subset relevan untuk fitur ini; detail lengkap pada Gambar 4.X.1 Class Diagram Manajemen User."
- Effort: small (one extra caption line per file)
- Academic impact: MEDIUM

**G6. Association navigability ambiguous**
- All non-composition relations use S_ASSOC (no arrows). User-AuditLog, User-Client, etc.
- TA examiners often ask "siapa yang navigate ke siapa?"
- Fix: For directional "created_by" / "changed_by" / "uploaded_by" relations, switch to S_ASSOC with `endArrow=open` or open triangle. Compositions and aggregations stay as-is.
- Effort: small (style edit in _gen.py)
- Academic impact: MEDIUM

### NICE-TO-HAVE (defendable to skip, mention as future work)

**G7. Three-tier stereotypes <<boundary>><<control>><<entity>>**
- ICONIX-style robustness diagram or full design CD with 3 layers
- Code reality is Express routes + React components, not classical MVC. Forced fit.
- Recommendation: SKIP. Justify in narrative: "model class diagram pada bab ini fokus pada lapisan entitas (domain model) sesuai konvensi pemodelan sistem informasi. Pemisahan boundary dan controller terjabarkan implisit pada arsitektur tier (Bab 3) dan sequence diagram (4.3.1.3)."
- Effort: 2-3 weeks if added
- Academic impact: LOW for SI thesis, MEDIUM for TI thesis

**G8. Package / Component / Deployment diagrams**
- TODO.md outline does NOT list these in 4.3.1.x
- Per UTY thesis template (verify with supervisor): Sistem Informasi typically only requires Class+ERD; deployment optional in Bab 3 or appendix
- Recommendation: SKIP unless supervisor explicitly requests. Mention "rancangan deployment dijelaskan secara naratif" in Bab 3.
- Effort: medium if added
- Academic impact: LOW (nice rigor, not required)

**G9. Object diagram (instance snapshot)**
- Useful for showing "1 Project with 3 Tasks at 2 phases" example
- Not standard in TA outline
- Recommendation: SKIP

**G10. STANDARD.md missing Class Diagram section**
- File covers Flowchart, Activity, Use Case, PlantUML -- but NO Class Diagram entry
- Fix: Add Class section to STANDARD.md documenting current style (swimlane+stackLayout shape, S_CLASS/S_ROW/S_SEP styles, composition/aggregation/association arrows). Locks convention for future maintenance.
- Effort: small (document existing _gen.py patterns)
- Academic impact: LOW for thesis grade, HIGH for project hygiene

---

## Recommended Action Sequence (in order)

1. **Add User generalization** -> edit _gen.py to introduce abstract User + 3 subclasses, regenerate CD_MANAJEMEN_USER + CD_SISTEM
2. **Add UML Notes for business rules** -> 4-6 sticky notes (SPI thresholds, computed states, survey gate, role gate). Anchor to ProjectHealth, Task, Project
3. **Add navigability arrows** to directional associations (created_by, uploaded_by, changed_by, reported_by)
4. **Prune RPC-style methods** from entity classes (User.login -> remove; keep User.deactivate, User.changeRole)
5. **Annotate REF stub convention** in per-fitur captions
6. **Document Class section in STANDARD.md** to lock convention
7. **Fix caption numbering** when inserting into Bab 4 (mechanical pass)
8. **Justify domain-only choice** in Bab 4 prose introducing 4.3.1.4

After items 1-5: regenerate all 7 .drawio files via _gen.py.

---

## Cross-Diagram Consistency Check

CD_SISTEM uses FULL class variants (USER_FULL etc.). Per-fitur uses mix of FULL (the focus class) and REF (cross-fitur references). Class names consistent throughout. Composition/aggregation usage consistent (Project COMPOSES Task/Material/Budget; Client AGGREGATES Project). Multiplicities present on all edges.

Verdict on consistency: SOLID. The REF stripping is intentional and correct -- just needs caption acknowledgment (G5).

---

## What NOT to do

- DO NOT collapse to single CD_SISTEM only. The per-fitur diagrams aid chapter narrative when each Bab 4 subsection discusses one fitur.
- DO NOT add MVC layering. Forced fit on Express+React reality. Justification path (entity-only with sequence diagrams covering interaction) is cleaner.
- DO NOT add Package/Component/Deployment unless supervisor demands. Outside outline scope.
- DO NOT change drawio shape engine. Current swimlane+stackLayout renders correctly per spot-check of CD_SISTEM.drawio.

---

## Defense Posture for Examiner Q&A

When asked "kenapa class diagram dipisah per fitur?":
> "Untuk memudahkan pembahasan setiap fitur pada subbab terkait di Bab 4. Gambar 4.X (Class Diagram Sistem) menyajikan model lengkap, sedangkan Gambar 4.X.1..6 menyajikan fragmen per-fitur dengan stub atribut untuk kelas referensi silang."

When asked "kenapa hanya entity layer, tidak ada boundary/control?":
> "Pemodelan ini fokus pada domain model (lapisan entitas). Interaksi antara antarmuka, kontroler, dan entitas digambarkan pada sequence diagram subbab 4.3.1.3, sehingga pemisahan tier terjabarkan implisit melalui sequence."

When asked "kenapa tidak ada inheritance untuk User role?":
> [If G1 fixed]: "Generalisasi User -> Technician/Manager/Admin ada pada Gambar 4.X.1, mencerminkan perbedaan perilaku dan otorisasi per peran." [If NOT fixed]: weak position, fix it.

When asked "dimana aturan bisnis SPI dan overtime di diagram?":
> [If G2 fixed]: "Pada catatan UML yang dilampirkan ke kelas ProjectHealth dan Task." [If NOT fixed]: weak position, fix it.

summary: Caption uses literal 'Gambar 4.X' placeholder -- needs mechanical numbering pass before thesis submission
User class lacks role-based generalization despite CHECK constraint role IN technician/manager/admin in schema
ProjectHealth class missing UML Notes for SPI thresholds (green>=0.95, amber 0.85-0.95, red<0.85)
STANDARD.md has no Class Diagram section -- convention exists only in _gen.py code, not documented

content:
User approved full critical+important fix list (G1-G7). Skipping G8-G10 nice-to-have per Yamamoto verdict.

Implementation scope:
- G1: User generalization (abstract User -> Technician/Manager/Admin)
- G2: UML Note helper + 4-6 business-rule notes (SPI thresholds, computed states, survey gate)
- G3: Dashed dependency arrowhead fix (endArrow=open instead of none)
- G4: <<enumeration>> classes for: task_status, user_role, project_phase, project_status, file_type, escalation_priority, etc.
- G5: Prune RPC methods on User (login, authenticate, etc.)
- G6: Navigability + role names on directional plain associations
- G7: Italic subtitle on per-fitur captions explaining REF subset convention

Spawning Nakamura. Output: edit _gen.py + regenerate 7 files. Validate after.

summary: TaskStatus has 5 values: to_do, in_progress, working_on_it, review, done — not 3. Enum class must reflect actual schema.

content:
## G1-G7 Implementation Complete

### Schema findings (verified from schema.sql before writing)

| Enum | Values confirmed |
|------|-----------------|
| Role | technician, manager, admin |
| TaskStatus | to_do, in_progress, working_on_it, review, done (5 values — spec assumed 3) |
| ProjectStatus | active, completed, on-hold, cancelled (not planning/on_hold/cancelled as spec assumed) |
| ProjectPhase | survey, execution |
| HealthStatus | green, amber, red |
| EscalationStatus | open, in_review, resolved (not in_progress/closed) |
| EscalationPriority | low, medium, high, critical |
| EvidenceFileType | photo, document, form, screenshot, other (not image/document/video) |
| ActivityType | arrival, start_work, pause, resume, note, photo, complete |

Dropped from spec: `depends_on` FK on Task (not in schema.sql), `action_request`/`action_request_status` on Escalation (not in schema.sql).

### New style constants added

- `S_CLASS_ABSTRACT` — fontStyle=3 (italic+bold) for abstract User header
- `S_ASSOC_DASH` — fixed G3: endArrow=open;endFill=0;endSize=12;dashed=1 (was endArrow=none)
- `S_ASSOC_DIR` — G6: endArrow=open;endFill=0;endSize=12 navigable association
- `S_GENERAL` — G1: endArrow=block;endFill=0;endSize=16 generalization
- `S_NOTE` — G2: shape=note;fillColor=#FFF8DC classic post-it note
- `S_NOTE_ANCHOR` — G2: dashed;endArrow=none note anchor line
- `S_SUBTITLE` — G7: fontSize=10 italic subtitle for REF diagrams

### New helpers added

- `cls(..., abstract=False)` — extended to support S_CLASS_ABSTRACT
- `enum_cls(cid, name, literals, x, y, w)` — G4: enumeration class with <<enumeration>> stereotype row
- `note(nid, x, y, w, h, text)` — G2: UML Note shape
- `note_anchor(eid, src, tgt)` — G2: dashed anchor edge
- `general_edge(eid, subclass_id, superclass_id, ...)` — G1: hollow triangle generalization
- `subtitle(text, x, y, w)` — G7: REF subset subtitle
- `emit_diagram()` — extended with notes, enum_classes, extra_edges, ref_subtitle params

### Cell count delta

| File | Was | Now | Delta | Main additions |
|------|-----|-----|-------|----------------|
| CD_MANAJEMEN_USER | 39 | 61 | +22 | abstract User, 3 subclasses, Role enum, gen edges, note |
| CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN | 33 | 33 | 0 | G6 dir edge + subtitle (replaced old edge) |
| CD_MANAJEMEN_PROYEK | 82 | 119 | +37 | 3 enums, 2 notes, dir edges, subtitle |
| CD_MANAJEMEN_TUGAS | 92 | 123 | +31 | 2 enums, 2 notes, dir edges, subtitle |
| CD_BUDGET_MATERIAL | 63 | 73 | +10 | dir edge reporter, user ref class, subtitle |
| CD_ESKALASI | 69 | 87 | +18 | 2 enums, note, dir edges, subtitle |
| CD_SISTEM | 279 | 405 | +126 | 3 subclasses, 8 enums, 3 notes, gen edges, all dir edges |

### Validation: 23/23 structural checks PASS
- All 7 files parse as valid XML
- G1: fontStyle=3 abstract header present, gen edges present
- G2: shape=note present, note anchors present, business rules text present
- G3: endArrow=open on S_ASSOC_DASH confirmed
- G4: all enum class names present in relevant files
- G5: login() method absent from User in CD_MANAJEMEN_USER
- G6: role labels (assignee, creator, reporter, uploader) present
- G7: REF subtitle text present in per-fitur diagrams

### Decisions made for ambiguous spec items

1. TaskStatus: spec said 3 values (to_do, working_on_it, done). Schema has 5. Used schema values (to_do, in_progress, working_on_it, review, done). Evidence-based.
2. EvidenceFileType: spec said image/document/video. Schema CHECK shows photo/document/form/screenshot/other. Used schema values.
3. EscalationStatus: spec said open/in_progress/resolved/closed. Schema shows open/in_review/resolved. Used schema values.
4. ProjectStatus: spec said planning/in_progress/on_hold/completed/cancelled. Schema shows active/completed/on-hold/cancelled. Used schema values.
5. depends_on FK on Task: in _gen.py class definition but NOT in schema.sql. Removed from TASK_FULL definition.
6. action_request/action_request_status on Escalation: was in old _gen.py ESCALATION tuple, NOT in schema.sql. Removed.
7. EscalationUpdate: spec calls this separate table. Schema has no escalation_updates table. Kept in diagram (may be planned), but noted as not yet in schema.
8. CD_MANAJEMEN_KLIEN: ref_subtitle=True applied even though it only has USER_REF (subset reference). Subtitle covers both REF usages.
